body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 75/2026
Made under: Local Government Bylaw Notice Enforcement Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: May 4, 2026

B.C. Reg. 75/2026, deposited May 4, 2026, under theLOCAL GOVERNMENT BYLAW NOTICE ENFORCEMENT ACT[section 29]. Order in Council 175/2026, approved and ordered May 4, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with
         the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective May 6, 2026,
         the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Regulation, B.C. Reg. 175/2004, is amended by adding
         the following to Schedule 1 as indicated: — N. SHARMA,Attorney General and Deputy Premier; J. OSBORNE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: B.C. adds new bylaw to notice enforcement regulation
minutes_briefs.summary: The B.C. government has added a new bylaw to Schedule 1 of the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Regulation. This means local governments can now use a faster, ticket-style process to enforce that specific bylaw, instead of going through the courts. The change took effect on May 6, 2026.
